Looking at the orchestration patterns in the diagram, which pattern would best address this quality problem?

options.text: Sequential — pass each receipt through a chain of specialized agents (OCR Agent, Categorization Agent, Filing Agent) to divide the extraction work into focused, ordered steps
Parallel — process all receipts simultaneously across multiple independent agents to reduce the processing bottleneck and catch more errors through separate verification
Loop — have an extraction agent process the receipt, then a reviewer agent check the results, and if errors are found, send it back for re-extraction until thresholds are met
Parallel with section-splitting — divide each receipt into separate zones (header, line items, total) and assign a different agent to process each zone simultaneously

questions.stem: Beacon's Day 5 email triage system uses a single AI with a system prompt to classify emails. On Day 7, this is upgraded to a two-agent system with a Triage Agent and a Quality Checker. Why do agents need capabilities beyond what a system prompt alone provides?

options.text: A single AI with a system prompt cannot verify its own work or pass results to another agent for review — agents add collaboration, tool access, and persistent context
System prompts are unable to produce structured output formats like JSON, which means agents are necessary for any task that requires classification or data extraction
System prompts have a strict 100-token limit on instruction length, so agents are required to handle longer or more detailed behavioral specifications for complex tasks
Agents connect to more advanced AI model tiers that offer higher accuracy and are not available through standard system prompt configurations in AI Studio

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group's marketing team uses AI to produce weekly promotional emails. The process has three steps: (1) a copywriter agent drafts the email body, (2) a compliance agent checks for regulatory issues in the draft, and (3) a formatting agent converts the approved text into the HTML email template. The compliance agent cannot begin until the draft exists, and formatting cannot begin until compliance clears the text. Referring to the orchestration patterns in the diagram, which pattern fits this workflow?

options.text: Sequential — pass each agent's output to the next in a fixed order, ensuring compliance reviews a complete draft and formatting receives approved text
Loop — have the copywriter agent regenerate drafts repeatedly until the compliance agent finds zero regulatory issues, then skip the formatting step entirely
Parallel — run all three agents at the same time so each agent works on its own specialty independently, then merge all outputs into the final email
Parallel with merge — run copywriting and compliance agents simultaneously on separate draft versions, then have the formatting agent reconcile any differences between them

questions.stem: During Beacon's Black Friday sale, the customer service team receives 5,000 support emails in four hours. Each email must be classified as a return request, a shipping inquiry, or a product question. No email's classification depends on any other email. Using the orchestration patterns shown in the diagram, which pattern best handles this situation, and why?

options.text: Sequential — process emails one at a time so each classification benefits from context gained by reviewing the previous email's content and resolution
Loop — classify each email, have a reviewer check the classification, and reclassify any errors, prioritizing accuracy over speed during a high-volume event
Parallel — run multiple classification agents simultaneously because each email can be categorized independently, reducing a four-hour backlog to minutes
Sequential with batching — group emails by store location first, then process each group sequentially so store-specific patterns improve classification accuracy

questions.stem: Beacon's legal team uses AI to draft vendor contracts, but the first drafts frequently contain vague payment terms and missing liability clauses. The team wants an AI workflow that automatically improves draft quality before a human lawyer reviews the final version. Referring to the orchestration patterns shown in the diagram, which approach best addresses this problem?

options.text: Sequential — pass the draft through a Payment Terms Agent, then a Liability Agent, then a Formatting Agent, ensuring each specialized agent contributes its section in order
Loop — have a drafting agent generate the contract, a reviewer agent check for vague terms and missing clauses, and send it back for revision until quality thresholds are met
Parallel — run a Payment Terms Agent and a Liability Agent simultaneously on the same draft, then merge their separate outputs into one consolidated contract document
Sequential with escalation — draft the contract, check for errors, and if any are found, immediately escalate to the human lawyer rather than attempting automated correction

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group needs to process 50 vendor contracts for its 25 stores. For each contract, AI must: (1) extract key terms, (2) check those terms against a compliance checklist, and (3) generate a summary report. Each step depends on the previous step's output, but different contracts are completely independent of each other. Which orchestration design handles this workflow most efficiently?

options.text: Loop — extract terms from all 50 contracts, review the full batch for compliance errors, and if any contract fails, reprocess the entire batch from the beginning
Sequential only — process each contract through all three steps one at a time, completing one contract fully before starting the next, to avoid mixing data between contracts
Parallel only — run extraction, compliance, and summary agents simultaneously on each contract so all three steps execute at the same time for maximum speed
Sequential pipeline per contract, run in parallel across contracts — each contract flows through extract, then compliance, then summary, but all 50 contracts are processed simultaneously

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group is building an AI agent to handle incoming supplier invoices. The agent should read each invoice PDF, verify the charges match the original purchase order, and either approve the invoice for payment or flag discrepancies for the accounts payable team. Using the perception-reasoning-action framework in the diagram, which mapping correctly applies the framework to this process?

options.text: Perception: compare invoice totals against purchase order amounts to find mismatches. Reasoning: extract individual line items from the invoice PDF document. Action: approve matching invoices and flag mismatches for the accounts payable team.

Perception: extract data from the invoice PDF and the matching purchase order. Reasoning: compare line items, quantities, and totals to identify discrepancies. Action: approve the invoice for payment or flag it with discrepancy details.

Perception: create a new purchase order based on the incoming invoice details. Reasoning: determine which vendor offers the lowest price for each line item. Action: send a revised purchase order back to the supplier.

Perception: approve the invoice if the total falls below a set threshold amount. Reasoning: categorize the expense by department and cost center code. Action: extract the vendor name, invoice number, and payment terms.

questions.stem: A marketing agency needs an AI agent to draft press releases for clients. The drafts must be accurate, on-brand, and polished. The agency wants to minimize the need for human editing. Which agent configuration best applies agent governance principles?

options.text: An agent with no specific instructions, relying on the model's general knowledge of press release formats and standard journalistic writing conventions
An agent that generates 20 variant drafts of each press release and randomly selects one to send to the client without any quality review step
A fully autonomous agent that writes and distributes press releases directly to media outlets without requiring human review, prioritizing speed over accuracy
An agent with a defined press-writer role, scope limited to approved client information, mandatory human approval before delivery, and version logging for audit

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group runs three independent nightly AI tasks: (1) optimize delivery routes for 200 trucks across 25 stores, (2) forecast next-week inventory demand for 5,000 SKUs, and (3) generate shift schedules for 400 store employees. The operations manager wants all three results ready by 6 AM. Currently, running them sequentially takes 5 hours and finishes at 4 AM. Which orchestration change and its justification are correct?

options.text: Switch to loop — iterate each task multiple times to reduce processing duration, since repeated runs execute faster as the model warms up its cached responses
Add a loop around the sequential pipeline — run all three tasks in order, then check for cross-task consistency, and repeat the full sequence if any conflicts exist
Switch to parallel — since the three tasks share no dependencies, running them simultaneously reduces total wall-clock time to roughly the duration of the longest single task
Keep sequential but reorder — put the fastest task first so partial results are available earlier, giving downstream tasks a head start on processing shared data

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group uses a sequential pipeline to process online returns: a Validation Agent checks the return request, then a Refund Agent calculates the amount, then a Communication Agent emails the customer. The CTO notices that 15% of return emails contain incorrect refund amounts because the Refund Agent occasionally miscalculates. Which modification to the orchestration pattern best addresses this quality issue without eliminating the speed benefits of the current design?

options.text: Replace the sequential pipeline with parallel processing so the three agents independently handle each return, then select the best result from their outputs
Add a loop around the Refund Agent step — a verification agent checks the calculation, and if errors are found, the Refund Agent recalculates before passing to Communication
Remove the Refund Agent and have the Communication Agent calculate refund amounts directly, reducing the number of handoffs where data can become corrupted between agents
Switch to a fully looped pipeline where the three steps repeat from the beginning whenever any single agent produces output that differs from its previous iteration

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group currently uses a single AI call to generate weekly sales summaries for its 25 stores, but the summaries sometimes misstate year-over-year growth percentages. A consultant proposes splitting this into two agents: a Data Analysis Agent that calculates the figures and a Narrative Agent that writes the summary using the Analysis Agent's verified numbers. Using the single-LLM vs. multi-agent comparison in the diagram, which analysis best explains why this two-agent design reduces errors?

options.text: Two agents process the sales data in half the time because each agent handles only part of the workload, and faster processing inherently produces fewer calculation mistakes
The Narrative Agent automatically corrects any errors in the Data Analysis Agent's output because the second agent in a pipeline has access to the full conversation history
The Data Analysis Agent uses a more powerful model than the Narrative Agent, so choosing a premium model tier for calculations substantially eliminates the risk of numerical errors
Each agent can be optimized for a single task — calculation accuracy vs. clear writing — so errors in one domain do not compound with errors in the other domain

questions.stem: A university considers three approaches for an AI that reviews student essays: (A) a single AI call that grades and provides feedback, (B) a sequential two-agent pipeline where one agent grades and another provides feedback, and (C) a loop pattern where the feedback agent reviews the grading agent's work and requests re-grading if the rubric was applied incorrectly. When is approach C (loop) clearly preferable to approach B (sequential)?

options.text: When grading accuracy is critical and the cost of incorrect grades outweighs the additional API costs of iterative refinement
When the university has only one AI model available, since loops can only use a single model while sequential requires two different models
When the university wants to minimize costs, since loops use fewer total tokens than sequential pipelines
When processing speed is the top priority, since loops complete faster than sequential pipelines

questions.stem: A startup builds a customer onboarding system with three agents: a Welcome Agent (greets users), a Setup Agent (configures accounts), and a Training Agent (walks users through features). The agents run in parallel to save time. During testing, users report confusion because the Training Agent references account settings that the Setup Agent has not yet configured. Evaluate the architectural fitness of this design.

options.text: The parallel pattern is wrong here because the Training Agent depends on the Setup Agent's output, so these agents should run sequentially
The parallel pattern is fine, but each agent needs a longer system prompt to account for the possibility that other agents have not finished
The design is correct and the confusion is caused by users failing to process the agents' outputs in the intended reading order
The problem is that three agents are too many for onboarding; combining them into a single agent would eliminate the timing confusion entirely

questions.stem: Beacon's development team proposes using the loop pattern for email triage: the Triage Agent classifies, the Quality Checker reviews, and if they disagree, the Triage Agent reclassifies up to 3 times. A manager asks whether this is the right pattern choice for 6,000 emails per month. Which judgment is most sound?

options.text: The loop pattern is inappropriate because AI agents have difficulty incorporating feedback within a single interaction to improve their reclassification accuracy
The loop pattern should be applied to the full volume of 6,000 emails because consistency requires that each email go through an identical review process
The loop pattern is the strongest choice because prioritizing accuracy over cost savings is the correct tradeoff for any classification task at scale
The loop provides highest accuracy but multiplies cost; for routine triage where most classifications are straightforward, sequential with human review of disagreements is more cost-effective

questions.stem: A healthcare system deploys a three-agent pipeline for patient discharge planning: Medical Summary Agent, Insurance Verification Agent, and Discharge Instructions Agent. The system has no human-in-the-loop checkpoint. Assess the governance adequacy of this deployment.

options.text: Adequate: AI agents in healthcare settings are already governed by HIPAA regulations, which provide sufficient oversight without requiring additional human review checkpoints
Partially adequate: the Insurance Verification Agent needs human oversight, but the Medical Summary and Discharge Instructions agents handle routine tasks that do not require review
Adequate: the three-agent pipeline architecture provides built-in cross-verification because each downstream agent reviews and validates the previous agent's output

Critically inadequate: healthcare decisions require human oversight, especially for insurance verification and medical instructions; human review points are needed before discharge instructions reach patients

questions.stem: A company deploys an agent system where the Drafting Agent creates marketing copy and the Review Agent checks for brand compliance. The system places the human-in-the-loop checkpoint after both agents complete their work. A colleague suggests moving the checkpoint to between the two agents instead. Evaluate which placement is better.

options.text: Between agents is better because having a human review intermediate output ensures quality at each stage, even though it slows down the overall pipeline throughput
Both placements produce identical outcomes because the human reviewer evaluates the same content regardless of where in the pipeline the checkpoint is positioned
Human-in-the-loop checkpoints should be removed entirely from marketing copy workflows because they create bottlenecks that negate the speed benefits of automation
After both agents is better because the Review Agent catches most brand compliance issues automatically, so humans only need to review the final vetted output

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group wants to expand its AI capabilities beyond email triage by building a multi-agent system for their HR department to screen the 4,200 job applications they receive annually. Which multi-agent architecture design best applies the patterns learned in the course?

options.text: A single agent with a lengthy system prompt that handles resume parsing, candidate evaluation, and ranking in one call, prioritizing lower API costs over task specialization
Parallel agents where the Parser, Evaluator, and Ranker process each resume simultaneously and vote on the outcome, despite their tasks having sequential dependencies
A loop pattern where the same agent reads each resume five times to ensure consistent scoring before proceeding to the next resume in the application queue
Sequential pipeline: Parser extracts candidate data, Evaluator scores against job requirements, Ranker produces a shortlist with justifications, and a human reviews before sending to hiring managers

questions.stem: An e-commerce company wants an AI quality checker agent that reviews product descriptions written by another AI agent. The quality checker should verify factual accuracy, brand voice consistency, and SEO keyword inclusion. Which agent design best implements a quality checker?

options.text: A generic proofreading agent instructed to check for errors and suggest improvements, with no specific evaluation criteria or structured output requirements defined
An agent that measures character count and keyword density as its sole quality metrics, ignoring factual accuracy and brand voice consistency in its evaluation process
An agent that automatically rewrites any description it finds unsatisfactory, bypassing the original writing agent entirely and removing the human review opportunity

An agent with three explicit check categories (accuracy, voice, SEO), structured pass/fail output per category, escalation criteria for failures, and a rule flagging uncertain reviews for human decision

questions.stem: A financial services firm wants to design an agent governance framework for a loan approval AI system. The framework must address trust boundaries, human-in-the-loop, monitoring, and audit trail. Which design is most comprehensive?

options.text: Restrict the AI to data entry tasks only, requiring human loan officers to make every approval decision, which the firm argues eliminates the need for governance entirely
Grant the AI full decision authority for loans under $10,000 with no monitoring or audit logging, and disable it for larger loans to limit exposure without overhead
Set trust boundaries (human sign-off above $50K), require human review of rejections, monitor approval rates by demographic for bias, and log every decision with reasoning for regulatory audit
Give the AI unrestricted authority to approve or reject loans at any amount to maximize processing speed, with a random 5% monthly sample reviewed by compliance staff

questions.stem: Beacon Retail Group's CTO asks you to design an orchestration pattern for an AI system that handles their end-of-month financial close. The process requires: (1) gathering expense reports from 25 stores, (2) checking each report against company policy, (3) generating a consolidated financial summary, and (4) drafting a variance analysis comparing actual vs. budget. Steps 1-2 can process each store independently, but step 3 needs all 25 stores' results, and step 4 needs step 3's output. Which orchestration pattern best fits this workflow?

options.text: Sequential — each store goes through the four steps one at a time before the next store begins, ensuring complete validation at each stage before proceeding
Parallel — the four steps run simultaneously across the 25 stores, maximizing throughput by eliminating wait time between steps entirely
Loop — consolidate store data, run the variance analysis, and if variances exceed thresholds, reprocess the 25 stores from the beginning
Sequential-parallel hybrid — parallel agents process 25 stores simultaneously for steps 1-2, then a sequential handoff feeds results to consolidation (step 3) and variance analysis (step 4)
